assignment_context.title: Coming-of-Age Literary Analysis
assignment_context.student_prompt: Using evidence from the story, explain how ordinary objects become connected to memory, grief, and power.

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: The work shows limited or emerging evidence of the standard.

levels.label: Approaching
levels.description: The work shows partial evidence of the standard but is uneven, general, or incomplete.

levels.label: Meeting
levels.description: The work shows clear and sufficient evidence of the standard.

levels.label: Exceeding
levels.description: The work shows especially strong, precise, or sophisticated evidence of the standard.

student_feedback.heading: Feedback
student_feedback.assignment_prompt: Using evidence from the story, explain how ordinary objects become connected to memory, grief, and power.

focus_standard_feedback.rationale: Across the response, the evidence is relevant and usually connected to the interpretation, though some explanation could be more precise.

review_unit_observations.text: Your evidence is relevant and well chosen. The next revision step is to explain more directly how the quotation supports your claim.

comments.text: Your evidence is relevant and usually well chosen. To improve, make sure each quotation is followed by analysis that explains exactly how it supports your interpretation.

focus_standard_feedback.rationale: The response identifies an important idea about memory and grief, but the analysis sometimes stays general instead of developing how that idea grows across the story.

review_unit_observations.text: You name a meaningful central idea, but the explanation needs to show how the object you discuss develops that idea.

comments.text: You are moving toward a clear interpretation of the story's central idea. In revision, focus on explaining how specific details build that idea instead of only naming the idea.

focus_standard_feedback.rationale: The response has a clear claim and uses evidence to support it. The organization is understandable, though transitions could make the relationship among ideas clearer.

comments.text: Your claim gives the response a clear direction. Strengthen the argument by using transitions that show how each paragraph adds to the same overall interpretation.

next_steps: Before revising, choose one quotation and add two to three sentences explaining how the language of that quotation supports your claim.
Then reread your transitions to make sure each paragraph clearly connects back to the central interpretation.

privacy_note: This is a synthetic example. It contains no real student names, real student IDs, real student writing, real scanned work, real grades, or personally identifiable student information.
